title: OpenAI’s existential questions
abstract: On the latest episode of Equity, we discuss OpenAI's latest acquisitions and whether they address "two big existential problems" for the company.

title: The 12-month window
abstract: A lot of AI startups exist partly because the foundation models haven't expanded into their category yet. As many jokingly acknowledge, that won't last forever.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn put a customer satellite in the wrong orbit during its third launch
abstract: The first major failure of Blue Origin's new heavy-launch system could create delays to its ambition to help NASA and the Trump administration return to the Moon.

title: TechCrunch Mobility: Uber enters its assetmaxxing era
abstract: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your hub for the future of transportation and now, more than ever, how AI is playing a part.

title: Cracks are starting to form on fusion energy’s funding boom
abstract: Fusion startups and investors risk deepening fissures if some key disagreements aren't settled amicably.

title: VC Ron Conway says he has a ‘rare form of cancer’
abstract: Conway wrote that he “will be stepping back from some of my usual activities,” but he will “continue to support” founders backed by his firm SV Angel.

title: AI chip startup Cerebras files for IPO
abstract: In recent months, the company announced an agreement with Amazon Web Services to use Cerebras chips in Amazon data centers, as well as a deal with OpenAI reportedly worth more than $10 billion.

title: Anthropic’s relationship with the Trump administration seems to be thawing
abstract: Despite recently being designated a supply-chain risk by the Pentagon, Anthropic is still talking to high-level members of the Trump administration.

title: The App Store is booming again, and AI may be why
abstract: New data from Appfigures shows a swell of new app launches in 2026, suggesting AI tools could be fueling a mobile software boom.

title: Once close enough for an acquisition, Stripe and Airwallex are now going after each other
abstract: For most of its life, Airwallex and Stripe have mostly operated in different geographies, selling to different buyers. That's changing.

title: Sam Altman’s project World looks to scale its human verification empire. First stop: Tinder.
abstract: World, which has raised eyebrows (but also a lot of interest) with its Orb-centered anonymous verification project, is looking to expand its influence via a bevy of new partnerships.

title: Kevin Weil and Bill Peebles exit OpenAI as company continues to shed ‘side quests’
abstract: Kevin Weil and Bill Peebles are leaving OpenAI as the company shuts down Sora and folds its science team, signaling a sharp pivot away from consumer moonshots toward enterprise AI.

title: Sources: Cursor in talks to raise $2B+ at $50B valuation as enterprise growth surges
abstract: Returning backers a16z and Thrive are expected to lead the round.

title: Hackers are abusing unpatched Windows security flaws to hack into organizations
abstract: A security researcher published details of three security vulnerabilities in Windows Defender, and the code used to exploit them. Now, hackers are taking advantage of the vulnerabilities in real-life attacks, according to a cybersecurity firm.

title: Harshita Arora Joins YC as General Partner
abstract: We're thrilled to announce that Harshita Arora is joining Y Combinator as a General Partner. She brings deep fintech and infrastructure experience, a founder's instinct for product, and the perspective of someone who's been building companies since she was a teenager.

title: Adding Canada Back to Our List of Accepted Countries of Incorporation
abstract: After hearing feedback from Canadian founders in our network, we are adding Canada back to our list of accepted countries of incorporation.

title: Congratulations to EquipmentShare on Going Public
abstract: Today, EquipmentShare (YC W15) goes public. From Missouri to 373 locations nationwide, they've built the operating system construction has been missing—built by contractors, for contractors. Congrats to Jabbok, Willy, and the EquipmentShare team on a huge milestone.

title: Announcing the YC AI Stack
abstract: We've partnered with nearly two dozen companies to offer students who attend YC events a suite of over $25,000 in free credits for AI devtools.

title: 2026 Demo Day Dates
abstract: We’re excited to share the Demo Day dates for 2026 so founders, investors, and our broader community can plan ahead.

title: Meesho Goes Public
abstract: Meesho goes public today. From S16 to IPO, they’ve built one of India’s largest e-commerce platforms—empowering millions of small merchants to grow their businesses. Congrats to Vidit and Sanjeev on a decade of relentless building and listening to users!

title: Congratulations to Groww
abstract: Today, Groww, India's leading digital investing platform, goes public. Tens of millions use Groww to invest simply and confidently in stocks, mutual funds, and ETFs. As YC's first company to list in India, we couldn't be more proud.

title: BillionToOne Goes Public — The Startup That Made Genetic Testing Universal
abstract: Today, BillionToOne goes public, becoming YC's fourth publicly traded biotech company. More importantly, they have built one of the most important genetic tests in the U.S. Today, 1 in 11 babies born in the US are tested with BillionToOne's fetal genetic test.

title: Meet YC's Newest Visiting Partners
abstract: We're excited to announce that nine exceptional founders are joining us as Visiting Partners at YC: Matt Riley, Harshita Arora, Grey Baker, Christopher Golda, Raphael Schaad, Christina Gilbert, Francois Chaubard, Vivian Shen, and James Evans.

title: YC x Coinbase RFS: Build Onchain
abstract: We are entering the era of Fintech 3.0. Regulatory clarity, growing consumer adoption, and low-cost chains have paved the way for a golden age of building in crypto — and at YC, Base, and Coinbase, we want to fund builders to seize this moment.

title: Ankit Gupta Joins YC as General Partner, Bringing Deep ML Expertise
abstract: We’re thrilled to announce that Ankit Gupta is joining YC as our newest General Partner. Ankit has already worked with dozens of YC founders as a visiting partner in recent batches. He has a rare blend of deep machine learning expertise and firsthand startup experience that make him

title: Dalton Caldwell’s Move to Partner Emeritus
abstract: After 12+ years, 25 batches, and working with more than a thousand startups, Dalton Caldwell is transitioning to Partner Emeritus at YC. Dalton joined YC in 2011 and has contributed in a number of roles over the years, most recently as a Managing Partner. During his time here, he worked

title: Welcoming Jon Xu and Andrew Miklas as YC’s Newest General Partners
abstract: Over the last few batches, visiting partners Jon Xu and Andrew Miklas have become two of the most trusted voices in the YC community. They were both founders in YC’s Summer 2010 batch, and now, 15 years and two industry-shaping companies later, they’re back in a new role: General Partners at YC.

title: Tyler Bosmeny built Clever into a $500M company–now he’s helping YC founders do the same as General Partner
abstract: We’re thrilled to welcome Tyler Bosmeny back to YC, this time as a General Partner.

title: Michael Seibel's Legacy Continues at YC: Transition to Partner Emeritus
abstract: If you’ve been around YC, you know Michael Seibel has been a huge part of it for over a decade. He’s done it all - YC Founder (w07, w12), Group Partner, and Managing Director/CEO of the YC accelerator. Along the way, he’s helped

title: Truecaller faces mounting pressures as its growth matures
abstract: As growth slows, Truecaller is leaning on subscriptions, business services, and new features to sustain momentum beyond India.

title: Anthropic created a test marketplace for agent-on-agent commerce
abstract: In a recent experiment, Anthropic created a classified marketplace where AI agents represented both buyers and sellers, striking real deals for real goods and real money.

title: Maine’s governor vetoes data center moratorium
abstract: L.D. 307 would have imposed the country’s first statewide moratorium on new data centers — lasting, in this case, until November 1, 2027.

title: OpenAI CEO apologizes to Tumbler Ridge community
abstract: In a letter to the residents of Tumbler Ridge, Canada, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said he is “deeply sorry” that his company failed to alert law enforcement about the suspect in a recent mass shooting.

title: The climate tech IPO window could finally be cracking open
abstract: Nuclear startup X-energy went public, geothermal startup Fervo is about to. Could this be the moment that climate tech investors have been waiting for?

title: Why Cohere is merging with Aleph Alpha
abstract: Canadian AI startup Cohere is taking over Germany-based Aleph Alpha with support from Lidl’s owner, Schwarz Group. With the blessing of their governments, the companies intend to offer a sovereign alternative to enterprises in an AI landscape dominated by American players.

title: Why Tokyo is the most important tech destination of 2026
abstract: SusHi Tech Tokyo 2026 has four tightly defined technology domains, each backed by live demonstrations, dedicated exhibit floors, and sessions featuring the people actually building and funding these technologies globally.

title: Lachy Groom to back India startup Pronto at a $200M valuation, sources say
abstract: This round, should it occur, would double the house-help startup's valuation in a matter of weeks.

title: Steve Ballmer blasts founder he backed who pleaded guilty to fraud: ‘I was duped and feel silly’
abstract: Steve Ballmer wrote a fiery letter in the sentencing of disgraced founder Joseph Sanberg documenting all the harm that's befalling him as an investor.

title: Cohere acquires, merges with Germany-based startup to create a ‘transatlantic AI powerhouse’
abstract: Cohere, the Canada-based AI company that makes AI tools for businesses in regulated industries, announced Friday it would merge with Aleph Alpha, a German company that also builds AI systems for businesses and governments.

title: India’s Snabbit closes $56M round as investor interest in on-demand home services heats up
abstract: Snabbit now processes over 40,000 daily jobs and has cut costs sharply as it expands across cities and services.

title: OpenAI ends Microsoft legal peril over its $50B Amazon deal
abstract: OpenAI has won major concessions from its largest shareholder, Microsoft, that will allow it to sell products on AWS, while Microsoft gets more cash in a revenue-share agreement.

title: DeepMind’s David Silver just raised $1.1B to build an AI that learns without human data
abstract: Ineffable Intelligence, a British AI lab founded a mere few months ago by former DeepMind researcher David Silver, has raised $1.1 billion in funding at a valuation of $5.1 billion.

title: Investors back Skye’s AI home screen app for iPhone ahead of launch
abstract: Skye's new AI app attracted investors before it even launched — a sign of interest in a more AI-aware iPhone.

title: China blocks Meta’s $2B Manus deal after months-long probe
abstract: China has ordered Meta to unwind its multibillion-dollar Manus acquisition, dealing a potential setback to Zuckerberg’s push into AI agents.

title: OpenAI could be making a phone with AI agents replacing apps
abstract: There have been plenty of rumors about OpenAI's hardware plans, which involve launching a pair of earbuds. A new note from industry analyst Ming-Chi Kuo suggests that the AI company might be working on a phone in collaboration with MediaTek, Qualcomm, and Luxshare.
